tax: Placed at family rank, Sleet Glacials is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a supercooled cloud body. A new genus belongs here only if it shows an airborne ice body with its own cold updraft.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a swirling hail-forming cloud. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — melting hailstones and ice-pocked stone — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Sleet Glacials occupies cold updraft shafts. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where water-pressure chills an updraft into a hail cloud and recur with the updraft re-chills, sustaining themselves on chilled updraft air.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: drifts in cold shafts, flings hail at intruders. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: cloud body and air-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: hail-shot and air-chilling. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Sleet Glacials matters because makes cold shafts a ranged ice hazard. It lets survey reports sort flung-ice hazards from creeping-frost ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Sleet Glacials is the family-rank grouping of airborne ice-forming elementals of the hail kind within the True Elementals. Its members share a supercooled cloud body that forms and flings hail, and divide below into genus groups by updraft chill and droplet load. The ranged cold hazard of the high shafts.
